questions.question: Still life photography is the art of photographing…
questions.reviewHint: Still life captures arranged inanimate objects, giving you full control of the scene.

questions.question: A big advantage of still life is that the photographer…
options: Has full control over arrangement, lighting and timing
Must shoot quickly before the subject moves
questions.reviewHint: Because objects don't move, you control composition, light and pace completely.

questions.question: Soft, diffused window light is popular in still life because it…
options: Creates gentle gradation and soft shadows
questions.reviewHint: Diffused natural light gives soft, flattering transitions and gentle shadows.

questions.question: Choosing objects that work well together is part of…
questions.reviewHint: Selecting complementary props and arranging them is core to still life styling.

questions.question: Colour theory helps still life by…
options: Guiding which colours harmonise or contrast in the arrangement
questions.reviewHint: Understanding colour relationships helps you build harmonious or deliberately contrasting setups.

questions.question: Creating balance and harmony in a still life arrangement means…
options: Distributing visual weight so the composition feels settled
Using only one light at maximum power
Keeping all objects the same size
questions.reviewHint: Balance and harmony come from how the objects' visual weights are arranged.

questions.question: A reflector in a still life setup is used to…
options: Fill shadows by bouncing light back into the scene
questions.reviewHint: Reflectors lift shadow detail by bouncing light back onto the arrangement.

questions.question: A tripod benefits still life because it…
options: Allows precise, repeatable framing and sharp images at slow shutter speeds
questions.reviewHint: A tripod lets you refine the arrangement precisely and shoot sharp at low ISO.

questions.question: Developing a consistent editing approach across still life images helps…
questions.reviewHint: Consistent post-processing gives a cohesive, signature look across a body of work.
